body:
## Summary

Fixes #442 — CI ran the full Rust test suite on changes that touch **no code at all** (docs-only commits, `.gitkeep` edits, changelog-fragment-only commits), wasting runner time and slowing iteration.

### Root cause

The `test` job in `.github/workflows/release.yml` gated on the wrong signal:

```yaml
if: ... || needs.changelog.result == 'success' || needs.changelog.result == 'skipped'
```

The `changelog` job is itself skipped **precisely when there are no code changes** (`any-code-changed == 'false'`). So the chain was:

```
no code changed → changelog SKIPPED → needs.changelog.result == 'skipped' is TRUE → test RUNS
```

`test` was the only change-sensitive job keying off a sibling job's `skipped` *result* instead of the `detect-changes` *outputs` that `lint` and `coverage` use.

**Proof:** [run 27463712189](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/27463712189) ran the full `cargo test` matrix for commit `19a07a58`, whose entire diff was a one-line deletion in `.gitkeep`. In that same run `Lint`, `Coverage`, `E2E`, and `Changelog` all correctly skipped.

### Fix

Decouple `test` from `changelog` and gate it on the change detector (the pattern the **python** template already uses correctly):

```yaml
test:
  needs: [detect-changes]
  if: |
    always() && !cancelled() && (
      github.event_name == 'push' ||
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.rs-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.toml-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'
    )
```

Non-code change → `test` skips → `build` (needs lint+test) also skips. Code change / push / dispatch → `test` runs. The changelog *check* itself is unchanged.

### How to verify

- Regression tests in `tests/unit/ci-cd/workflow_release.rs`:
  - `test_job_skips_non_code_changes` — pins the corrected gating, forbids the `needs.changelog.result == 'skipped'/'success'` clauses from returning.
  - `change_gated_jobs_never_depend_on_a_skipped_changelog` — generalizes the invariant to `lint`/`test`/`coverage`/`test-e2e-local`.
- Run: `cargo test --test unit -- ci_cd::workflow_release` → 18 passed.

### Template comparison & upstream reports (issue requirement)

Downloaded all four pipeline templates and compared the `test` gating. **python = correct reference**; **rust / csharp / js share the bug** (rust is the direct source of this repo's pipeline). Reported upstream with reproducible example + workaround + fix:

- rust: link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#71
- csharp: link-foundation/csharp-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#25
- js: link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#79

### Case study

`docs/case-studies/issue-442/` — downloaded logs/data, reconstructed timeline, full requirement list, root-cause analysis, solution plan, prior-art survey (`dorny/paths-filter`, native `paths` filters), and the upstream report links.

### Notes

- Existing detector tracing (`scripts/detect-code-changes.rs`) was already sufficient to confirm the root cause from the run log, so no extra verbose mode was needed; the new tests make the invariant executable instead.

Fixes #442

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/442

The test job ran whenever the changelog job was *skipped*, but changelog
is skipped precisely when there are no code changes — so docs-only,
.gitkeep, and changelog-fragment-only commits triggered the full
cargo test matrix (proven by run 27463712189 on a one-line .gitkeep
deletion).

Gate the test job on the detect-changes outputs (any-code-changed /
rs-changed / toml-changed / workflow-changed), the same way lint and
coverage already do, and decouple it from the changelog job.

Adds regression tests pinning the corrected gating and a case study
under docs/case-studies/issue-442. The same defect was found in the
rust/csharp/js pipeline templates and reported upstream.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(ci): stop running the test suite on non-code changes (#442)

title: fix(ci): stop running the test suite on non-code changes (#442)
